Title: Sulfur deficiency–induced repressor proteins optimize glucosinolate biosynthesis in plants

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPR-containing protein; sulfur deficiency induced protein; putative nuclear localization signal
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses glucosinolate accumulation, especially aliphatic glucosinolates, under sulfur deficiency and sulfur sufficiency; promotes prioritization of sulfur for primary metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout and double knockout analysis; overexpression under CaMV 35S; qRT-PCR; glucosinolate metabolite profiling; microarray/PCA; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC; transactivation assay; EMSA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDI1 is strongly induced by sulfur deficiency and OAS, mainly in roots and vascular tissues. 2. Loss of SDI1 increases aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic gene expression and glucosinolate accumulation, especially in sdi1sdi2 double mutants. 3. SDI1 overexpression represses BCAT4, MAM1, CYP79F2/CYP79F1, CYP83A1 and lowers glucosinolate levels. 4. SDI1 localizes to the nucleus/cytosol and physically interacts with MYB28 in planta. 5. SDI1 inhibits MYB28-mediated transcriptional activation of CYP79F1 and CYP83A1 without abolishing MYB28 DNA binding.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoots and roots; strong association with vascular tissues for pathway gene expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against pathogens and herbivores; medicinal/carcinopreventive and antibiotic activities
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by sulfur deficiency in roots; expressed in vascular tissues of roots and shoots, stems, flower buds, stigma, and stamens; not detected in mature siliques
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and vascular tissues under sulfur deficiency; induced by OAS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native SDI1 promoter for GFP/GUS reporter
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sulfur deficiency (15 µM sulfate versus 1500 µM sulfate); OAS treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced SDI activity may increase sulfur allocation to glucosinolates and decrease GSH/Cys pools; glucosinolate production has growth/energy cost
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing leaf SDI1 activity is suggested to increase or sustain health-beneficial glucosinolate content under low sulfur supply; caution due to altered primary sulfur metabolite balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the major SDI family member controlling glucosinolate repression; stronger effect than SDI2; acts through protein-protein interaction rather than direct DNA binding

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPR-containing protein; sulfur deficiency induced protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Additive repressor of glucosinolate biosynthesis, weaker than SDI1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; double knockout with sdi1; overexpression under CaMV 35S; qRT-PCR; glucosinolate profiling; promoter:GFP/GUS expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDI2 is induced by sulfur deficiency and OAS, mainly in roots and vascular tissues. 2. Single sdi2 mutants resemble WT more closely than sdi1, indicating weaker contribution. 3. SDI2 overexpression represses aliphatic glucosinolate pathway genes and glucosinolate accumulation, but less strongly than SDI1. 4. SDI2 acts additively with SDI1 in repressing glucosinolate biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by sulfur deficiency in roots; expression mainly in vascular tissues; generally lower than SDI1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots and vascular tissues under sulfur deficiency; induced by OAS
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native SDI2 promoter for GFP/GUS reporter
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced SDI2 together with SDI1 can lower GSH/Cys due to enhanced sulfur flux to glucosinolates
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be targeted together with SDI1 to enhance glucosinolate content, though effect is smaller alone
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SDI2 contribution is additive and weaker than SDI1; Arabidopsis Brassicaceae-specific subbranch suggests specialized function

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major positive regulator of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis; its activity is repressed by SDI1 under sulfur deficiency, leading to lower aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; transient transactivation assay using CYP79F1 and CYP83A1 promoters; EMSA; transcript profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB28 is a major transcription factor promoting aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis. 2. SDI1 physically interacts with MYB28 in yeast and in plant nuclei. 3. MYB28 activates CYP79F1 and CYP83A1 promoters. 4. SDI1 suppresses MYB28-mediated transactivation without preventing MYB28 binding to cis-elements. 5. MYB28 transcript level is negatively affected by SDI1 and participates in feedback circuitry with SDI genes.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoots and roots; associated with vascular tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in vascular tissues of leaves; up-regulated by sulfur deficiency in transcriptome context described here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis tissues where aliphatic glucosinolate genes are expressed
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for effector in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Sulfur deficiency indirectly via SDI1/SDI2; possible autoregulation and positive regulation network with MYB29 and MYB76
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic genes; activity inhibited by protein-protein interaction with SDI1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CYP79F1, CYP83A1 and other aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic genes including BCAT4 and MAM1
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP79F1; CYP83A1; BCAT4; MAM1; MYB29; MYB76; MYB28
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation of MYB28 and activation of MYB29/MYB76 reported; feedback with SDI1/SDI2 suggested
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major transcription factor that promotes aliphatic glucosinolate pathway and is the direct functional target of SDI1 repression under sulfur deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MYB28 activity likely increases sulfur and energy allocation to glucosinolate production
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating MYB28 together with SDI1/SDI2 could tune aliphatic glucosinolate content; caution for sulfur-use balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EMSA showed MYB28 DNA binding is maintained in SDI1-MYB28 complex; repression likely occurs after DNA binding, possibly via steric hindrance

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad regulator of sulfur deficiency responses linked upstream of SDI1 expression and glucosinolate down-regulation under sulfur deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Inference from previously published microarray analysis of slim1 mutants discussed in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLIM1 was previously identified as a transcription factor regulating broad sulfur deficiency responses. 2. SDI1 expression depends on SLIM1 status, suggesting SLIM1 acts upstream of SDI1. 3. The paper proposes a SLIM1-mediated pathway inducing SDI proteins, which then repress glucosinolate biosynthesis under sulfur deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Response regulation linking sulfur deficiency signaling to glucosinolate biosynthesis repression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not transcriptionally regulated by sulfur deficiency according to discussion
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Upstream transcriptional regulation of sulfur deficiency marker genes including SDI1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Affects SDI1 and sulfur deficiency response genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream sulfur deficiency regulator connecting sulfur status to SDI-mediated glucosinolate repression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Referenced microarray data in slim1 mutants; accession not provided in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as upstream contextual regulator rather than direct core experimental subject in the Results
